name: CrisisResponsePlan
title: Crisis Response Plan (CRP)
publisher: SPiER Project (FHIR translation of the Crisis Response Plan)
description: FHIR Questionnaire representation of the Crisis Response Plan (CRP), a brief, collaboratively-authored suicide-prevention intervention (Bryan & Rudd). The CRP is a personalized, patient-held plan — typically handwritten on a card — with five sections: personal warning signs, self-management/coping strategies, reasons for living, social support, and professional/crisis support. Completed collaboratively and persisted as a CarePlan (one activity per section), mirroring the SPiER Stanley-Brown Safety Plan encoding.
purpose: Establish a written, individualized crisis response plan the patient can use to manage a suicidal crisis. An alternative/complement to the Stanley-Brown Safety Plan at the Document Safety Actions stage. Produces a CarePlan, not a scored Observation.
copyright: The Crisis Response Plan (CRP) is a clinical intervention described by Craig J. Bryan and M. David Rudd (e.g., Brief Cognitive-Behavioral Therapy for Suicide Prevention). Used by SPiER under the licensing/permission held by the SPiER project; attribute to Bryan & Rudd.

item.text: Complete the Crisis Response Plan collaboratively with the patient. The patient keeps a copy (card, photo, or app). List one item per line; add as many as needed.
Warning signs — personal signs (thoughts, feelings, situations, behaviors) that a crisis may be developing
Self-management / coping strategies — things I can do on my own to calm and comfort myself
Reasons for living — what matters to me and is worth living for
Social support — people I can reach out to, and social settings that help me feel better
Professional & crisis support — clinician, 988 Suicide & Crisis Lifeline, Crisis Text Line (text HOME to 741741), and the nearest emergency department
